request: Plaintiff requests all emails mentioning 'the project' from the last five years.
reason: Overly broad and unduly burdensome; objection proper.

request: Defendant seeks the contract signed by both parties on 3 March.
reason: Directly relevant document; must be produced.

request: Party requests the opponent's internal legal advice memos.
reason: Protected by attorney-client privilege.

request: Request for the defendant's bank statements for the month of the alleged fraud.
reason: Narrow time frame and directly relevant.

request: Plaintiff demands every text message the defendant has ever sent.
reason: Unlimited scope; disproportionate and intrusive.

request: Request for the safety inspection report filed the week before the accident.
reason: Directly relevant to the claim; discoverable.

request: Party asks for the personal diary of the opposing CEO.
reason: Not relevant and invades personal privacy.

request: Request for the employment records of the employee involved in the incident.
reason: Relevant to the dispute; proper discovery.

request: Plaintiff requests all medical records of the defendant's family members.
reason: Third-party medical records; privacy and irrelevance.

request: Request for the training manual given to staff on the date of the incident.
reason: Relevant to standard of care; discoverable.

request: Party seeks settlement discussions from a prior unrelated case.
reason: Settlement negotiations are generally inadmissible.

request: Request for the building's CCTV footage from the day of the slip-and-fall.
reason: Direct evidence of the incident; must be preserved and produced.

request: Plaintiff demands the defendant's tax returns for the last 20 years.
reason: Overly broad; tax returns are sensitive and rarely discoverable.

request: Request for the product testing results conducted before the product launch.
reason: Directly relevant to a product liability claim.

request: Party requests draft versions of a contract that was never finalised.
reason: Drafts may be protected; not relevant unless bad faith alleged.

request: Request for the log of who entered the server room on the night of the breach.
reason: Narrow, relevant, and likely key evidence.

request: Plaintiff asks for all of the defendant's social media posts ever made.
reason: Overly broad; only posts relevant to the claim are discoverable.

request: Request for the police report filed regarding the incident in question.
reason: Public record and directly relevant.

request: Party requests the therapist's notes of a witness who is not a party to the case.
reason: Third-party privilege; not discoverable without compelling need.

request: Request for the company's harassment complaint filed by the plaintiff.
reason: Central to the claim; discoverable.

request: Plaintiff demands the home addresses of every employee in the company.
reason: Not relevant; privacy concerns and disproportionate burden.

request: Request for the quality control logs from the production line for the relevant month.
reason: Narrow scope and directly relevant to the defect claim.

request: Party seeks notes taken by the opposing lawyer during witness interviews.
reason: Work product doctrine protects lawyer's notes.

request: Request for the defendant's phone records for the hour of the accident.
reason: Narrow time frame; relevant to distraction or alibi.

request: Plaintiff requests all internal emails from every employee for a decade.
reason: Massively overbroad; unduly burdensome.

request: Request for the signed delivery receipt for the shipment in dispute.
reason: Key document; directly relevant and discoverable.

request: Party requests the unrelated criminal record of a witness.
reason: Generally inadmissible character evidence; not relevant to this civil matter.

request: Request for the environmental impact study filed before the construction permit.
reason: Relevant to the regulatory dispute; discoverable.

request: Plaintiff demands the defendant's browsing history for the past year.
reason: Overly broad and invasive; no shown relevance.

request: Request for the minutes of the board meeting where the decision was made.
reason: Directly relevant to the claim; proper discovery.
